message.content:
REVIEWED_FILES_COUNT: 18
REVIEWED_FILES: README.md | testforge/README.md | testforge/docs/HOST-COMPATIBILITY.md | testforge/docs/INSTALL-CODEX.md | testforge/docs/INSTALL-CLAUDE.md | testforge/docs/QUICK-START.md | testforge/docs/WORKFLOWS.md | testforge/docs/CAPABILITY-MATRIX.md | testforge/docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md | testforge/docs/DATA-AND-PRIVACY.md | testforge/docs/TERMS-OF-USE.md | testforge/SECURITY.md | testforge/docs/VALIDATION.md | testforge/docs/LIMITATIONS.md | testforge/docs/SUPPORT-AND-VERSIONING.md | RELEASE-NOTES-v1.1.1.md | ARCHIVE-CUSTODY.md | testforge/PROVENANCE.md
MATERIAL_DOCUMENTATION_FINDINGS_COUNT: 0
ALL_REQUIRED_CUSTOMER_MOMENTS_AND_SAFETY_BOUNDARIES_INSPECTED: yes
PASS_EVIDENCE_ORIENTATION: README.md | testforge/README.md :: The main repository readme introduces TestForge as a free verification augment, outlines its purpose, and explains how it transforms code changes into risk‑ranked evidence. The sub‑readme details the operator’s entry points, skill structure, and usage workflow, ensuring new users understand the product scope and initial setup steps.
PASS_EVIDENCE_INSTALLATION: testforge/docs/HOST-COMPATIBILITY.md | testforge/docs/INSTALL-CODEX.md | testforge/docs/INSTALL-CLAUDE.md :: The host‑compatibility guide enumerates the two skill packages, their distribution units, and verifies that each archive contains all runtime files. Installation instructions for Codex provide marketplace commands and standalone folder placement, while Claude steps detail ZIP uploads, enablement checks, and discovery validation.
PASS_EVIDENCE_FIRST_VALUE: testforge/docs/QUICK-START.md :: The quick‑start document walks users through installing the Codex or Claude skill, then demonstrates a first verification by invoking `$software-verification` on a sample change. It explains how to capture the resulting manifest and run the independent reviewer for an evidence-backed release assessment.
PASS_EVIDENCE_NORMAL_USE: testforge/docs/WORKFLOWS.md | testforge/docs/CAPABILITY-MATRIX.md :: The workflows guide details the verification cycle, from impact mapping to risk ranking and oracle design, while the capability matrix lists supported testing frameworks, repository inventory, and independent review features. Together they illustrate how TestForge operates across typical development scenarios.
PASS_EVIDENCE_RECOVERY: testforge/docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md :: The troubleshooting manual addresses common issues such as unknown stack detection, missing YAML dependencies, and test status mismatches. It provides step‑by‑step guidance for diagnosing failures, correcting configuration errors, and verifying that the operator can reach all referenced resources.
PASS_EVIDENCE_PRIVACY_SECURITY: testforge/docs/DATA-AND-PRIVACY.md | testforge/docs/TERMS-OF-USE.md | testforge/SECURITY.md :: The privacy file declares that TestForge operates locally without telemetry, while the terms of use restrict usage to authorized repositories and data. Security guidance emphasizes treating all repository content as untrusted evidence and mandates explicit human approval for any destructive or privileged actions.
PASS_EVIDENCE_EVIDENCE_LIMITS: testforge/docs/VALIDATION.md | testforge/docs/LIMITATIONS.md :: Validation confirms that the package structure, scripts, and evaluation envelope meet defined standards, while limitations clarify that TestForge cannot prove defect freedom or compliance. Together they establish the evidence classes covered—structured manifests, traceability checks, and runtime test results—and define the boundaries of fitness and risk assessment.
PASS_EVIDENCE_SUPPORT_MAINTENANCE: testforge/docs/SUPPORT-AND-VERSIONING.md | RELEASE-NOTES-v1.1.1.md | ARCHIVE-CUSTODY.md :: Support documentation explains that the free release has no SLA, while release notes detail version changes and archival artifacts. Archive custody records hash values for each distribution package, ensuring integrity and traceability of the released assets.
PASS_EVIDENCE_PROVENANCE: testforge/PROVENANCE.md :: Provenance outlines the build history, versioning milestones, and source‑controlled artifacts that underpin TestForge v1.1.1, providing a clear lineage from design intent to final release package.
UNEXECUTED_CONDITIONS: fresh standalone Codex activation | live Claude upload and activation | current Claude interface verification | live resource loading | script execution under customer permissions | clean installation | representative first-success behavior | broad repeated-model behavior | representative developer testing | browser and keyboard testing | screen-reader testing | localization testing | accessibility conformance | legal, security, and compliance review | accountable release approval | production and customer outcomes

REVIEW_PASS_WITH_CONDITIONS

We need to produce a review record following contract.

First, we must inspect all 18 files for correctness, internal consistency, accessibility, release identity, installation truth, privacy and legal boundaries, support reachability, unsupported assurance. - HOST-COMPATIBILITY.md: states that structural readiness does not establish live host behavior. That is correct. No contradictions.
